---
title: "Analyst Resume"
author: "Sonika Mahat"
output:
  pdf_document: default
---
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```
## PROFILE
I’m a graduate student in International Development who never thought of myself as a coder but I’ve come to really enjoy working with data. I especially love the process of cleaning messy datasets and turning them into clear, meaningful visuals that help tell important stories. Through my coursework and projects, I’ve built hands-on skills in R, data visualization, and statistical analysis. I’m excited about using these tools to explore complex challenges in international development and environment, and I’m looking for analyst roles where I can keep learning, asking questions, and sharing insights in a clear and impactful way.

##  TECHNICAL SKILLS  
* **Languages & Tools:** R, RMarkdown, Git, GitHub, Microsoft Excel, HTML
* **Data Wrangling & Analysis:** Comfortable using packages like dplyr, tidyr, readr, and stringr to clean, organize, and explore datasets
* **Visualization:** Enjoy using ggplot2 and patchwork to create clean, compelling visuals that help communicate key takeaways
* **Statistical Analysis:** Familiar with basic statistical techniques including linear regression, hypothesis testing, and chi-squared tests
* **Reporting & Reproducibility:** Experienced in using RMarkdown, posterdown, beamer, and GitHub Pages to share reproducible work clearly and professionally

##  RELEVANT PROJECTS
I don’t come from a technical or coding background and hadn’t planned to pursue work as an analyst but Data Analysis course really changed that for me. I’ve come to enjoy working with data, and after completing several challenging projects in class, I now see data analysis as a skill I want to keep building on. Some of the most intensive and rewarding projects from the course include:

**Gihub Page: SonikaMahat**

###  Research Poster: Urban Social Disorder (USD 3.0 Dataset)  
* Cleaned and analyzed urban protest data across 70+ countries using R
* Conducted a chi-squared test to assess the relationship between capital status and riot type
* Built a reproducible workflow in RMarkdown; designed and hosted an academic poster using posterdown and GitHub Pages
*  Presented results in a format that made sense to both technical and non-technical audiences

###  Slide Deck: Green Space Inequality in South Asia (Stowell, Jennifer. 2023. "Global Greenspace Indicator Dataset")  
* Used the Global Greenspace Indicator dataset to examine links between HDI levels and access to greenspace across cities
* Visualized patterns of inequality using bar graphs, scatterplots, and regression lines
* Created a professional slide deck using RMarkdown Beamer for class presentation

###  Problem Sets (x4) – SIS-750 Data Analysis  
* Topics included data wrangling, statistical inference, visualization, and custom functions
* Wrote clean, well-documented code in R to complete each assignment
* Learned how to make my work reproducible and easy to follow

##  EDUCATION
###  American University, Washington, DC
MA in International Development, *(2024–Present)*
Coursework: Data Analysis (SIS-750)

###  King’s College, Westcliff University 
MBA, *(2020–2022)*

###  Indraprastha College, University of Delhi 
B.Com, *(2015–2018)*
